choices.text: Model size + right-sizing. Default-to-biggest-model habit is the #1 cost mistake. Llama 70B costs 5-10x more to run than Llama 8B; if 8B handles your use case (often it does for 80-95% of queries), use it. Test on representative queries before committing. Cascading approach: try smallest first (Phi 3.5 Mini 3.8B), upgrade only when quality insufficient. Combined with: quantization (INT8/INT4 = 50-75% memory reduction; pack more inference per GPU); caching (repeated queries free); continuous batching (5-10x throughput per replica). Model right-sizing + quantization + caching together = 10-100x cost reduction with minimal quality impact for most workloads. The 'always use most powerful model' habit is leftover from when only GPT-4 worked; modern smaller models match GPT-3.5 quality at 1/10 cost. Pro habit: benchmark smaller models on your data; only upgrade when measurable quality gap matters. Cost optimization is mostly about not over-engineering, not about advanced optimization techniques.

questions.explanation: Model right-sizing. Default-to-biggest is #1 cost mistake. Try small first; upgrade only if quality insufficient. Combine with quantization + caching.

questions.prompt: AI chatbot serving 100K queries/day; API cost $1000/day. Cheaper alternative?

choices.text: Self-host on dedicated GPU. Cost math: $1000/day = $30K/month API cost. Self-hosted A10G GPU on cloud = ~$1/hour × 24h × 30 = $720/month per GPU. Single GPU handles 100K queries/day with continuous batching + appropriate model size. Savings: ~$29K/month. Setup: 1) Pick open-source model (Llama 3.1 8B / Mistral 7B / Phi 3.5) — verify commercial license. 2) Deploy on Inference Endpoint OR self-host on cloud GPU instance. 3) Use TGI/vLLM for continuous batching (boost throughput per replica). 4) Auto-scale based on load (probably 1-3 replicas peak; less off-peak). 5) Cache common queries (Redis). 6) Monitor latency + quality + cost. Break-even threshold: roughly 100K-1M queries/day depending on model + use case; above threshold, self-hosting saves; below, APIs win for ops simplicity. Trade-offs of self-hosting: 1) Engineering time for setup + ongoing ops. 2) Quality verification (you own the model output; not OpenAI's tuning). 3) Capacity planning (need to handle spikes). For 100K queries/day mature use case: worth investing in self-hosting. For 1K queries/day: API simpler + cheaper net of ops. Pro habit: model the costs at projected scale; switch when math justifies.

questions.explanation: Self-host open-source model on dedicated GPU. $1K/day API → ~$25/day GPU. Break-even ~100K-1M queries/day. Trade-off: engineering ops vs. savings.
